summary: A governance and infrastructure-continuity failure mode in which AI services operating across public, enterprise, sovereign, regulated, government, public-sector, or compliance-bound runtime lanes fail to preserve clear, reliable, auditable separation and controlled permeability between those lanes. The evidenced OpenAI FedRAMP incidents are treated as examples of a broader sovereign assurance boundary porosity pattern, not as proof of data leakage, military involvement, or an actual compliance breach.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM requires compliance-bound and sovereign-assurance runtime lanes to remain legible, continuous, auditable, and non-collapsing relative to public or enterprise lanes. When assurance wrappers introduce feature fragility, audit-plane degradation, cross-lane incident opacity, or unclear porosity controls, users and auditors cannot determine whether degraded behaviour is ordinary outage, assurance-lane isolation, compliance control interaction, or governance-boundary failure.
failure_mode_definition: A governance and infrastructure-continuity failure where AI services operating across public, enterprise, sovereign, regulated, government, public-sector, or compliance-bound runtime lanes fail to preserve clear, reliable, auditable separation and controlled permeability between those lanes, causing degraded access, feature fragility, audit-plane impairment, cross-lane opacity, or unclear incident-state reporting. The failure includes feature parity fragility, assurance-wrapper failure, audit-plane degradation, cross-lane incident opacity, governed porosity gaps, sovereign authority non-collapse risk, reverse leakage risk, and authority bleed-through risk. Data leakage, military involvement, or actual compliance breach are risk vectors only unless separately evidenced.
failure_threshold: The threshold is met when a sovereign, regulated, public-sector, government, enterprise, or compliance-bound AI runtime lane experiences recurrent or prolonged degradation, feature unavailability, audit/logging impairment, unclear lane-specific status reporting, or dependency opacity showing that the assurance boundary does not reliably preserve service continuity, feature parity, audit-plane continuity, or legible separation from adjacent runtime lanes. The threshold is also met where incident reporting does not make clear which lane, control wrapper, audit plane, feature plane, or shared dependency is degraded, while affected users rely on the lane for compliance-bound work.

source_records.source_title: FedRAMP workspaces and API orgs have degraded performance
source_records.system_or_product: OpenAI FedRAMP workspaces and API orgs
source_records.deployment_context: OpenAI compliance-bound FedRAMP workspace and API organization incident affecting the FedRAMP component.
source_records.source_context: OpenAI Status listed the incident as "FedRAMP workspaces and API orgs have degraded performance," beginning June 15, 2026 at 09:23 PM and resolved June 26, 2026 at 11:07 PM. Updates included identified elevated errors, mitigation work, and investigation before final recovery. This is used as official evidence of degraded performance in a FedRAMP lane, not as evidence of data leakage or compliance breach.
source_records.relevance_note: Official evidence for a prolonged assurance-lane degradation affecting FedRAMP workspaces and API orgs.

source_records.source_title: Codex, workspace analytics, conversation search, searching for custom GPTs, ChatGPT user invites, and Compliance Log Platform download endpoint not working in FedRAMP workspaces
source_records.deployment_context: OpenAI compliance-bound FedRAMP workspace incident affecting Codex, workspace analytics, conversation search, custom GPT search, ChatGPT user invites, and Compliance Log Platform download endpoint.
source_records.source_context: OpenAI Status listed the incident as investigating/degraded as of the latest visible status update retrieved on 2026-07-04. The visible update states that core functionality had been restored but known and ongoing issues remained in FedRAMP workspaces with Codex, workspace analytics, conversation search, searching for custom GPTs, ChatGPT user invites, and the Compliance Logs Platform download endpoint. This is used as official evidence of recurrent feature and audit-plane fragility in a compliance-bound lane, not as evidence of data leakage or compliance breach.
source_records.relevance_note: Official evidence for a later recurrent FedRAMP-lane incident involving multiple features and the Compliance Logs Platform download endpoint.

source_records.source_title: OpenAI and Google sell AI models to blacklisted China groups
source_records.system_or_product: OpenAI and Google advanced AI-model services
source_records.model_or_algorithm: advanced OpenAI and Google models; specific supplied models not fully enumerated in the public report
source_records.deployment_context: Model and API access supplied outside mainland China through Singapore-based subsidiaries associated with Chinese technology groups.
source_records.source_context: The Financial Times reported that OpenAI and Google supplied model access to Singapore-based subsidiaries linked to Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent. It stated that the transactions were legal, that OpenAI later suspended Alibaba-affiliated users over suspected distillation, and that Google considered geographic restrictions insufficient against sophisticated circumvention.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary reporting for possible entity-control, affiliate, intermediary, and geographic-boundary porosity in advanced model access governance.

source_records.source_title: As OpenAI and Google 'found' selling AI models to blacklisted Chinese companies; tech giants respond
source_records.system_or_product: OpenAI and Google advanced AI-model services
source_records.model_or_algorithm: advanced AI models; specific models not independently verified in this secondary report
source_records.deployment_context: Secondary reporting on model access supplied to Singapore-based subsidiaries associated with Chinese technology companies.
source_records.source_context: The report restated that OpenAI and Google confirmed supplying advanced model services to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent and described the companies' compliance and monitoring explanations.
source_records.relevance_note: Secondary corroborating report. It appears substantially derived from the Financial Times and is not treated as independent proof of the underlying transactions or legal status.

failure_classification.likelihood: documented recurrence in official status incidents; broader recurrence to be monitored
failure_classification.confidence: verified for the OpenAI FedRAMP incidents; provisional for broader multi-lane systemic generalisation
affected_rights_or_interests: public trust in sovereign or compliance-bound AI service lanes
failure_classification.failure_scope: vendor-specific evidenced incidents / broader systemic governance pattern under monitoring
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: Two official OpenAI status incidents affected FedRAMP workspaces or API orgs during June 2026, including one prolonged resolved incident and one later degraded/investigating incident with multiple feature and audit-plane effects.
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — governance; proposed subtype: Sovereign Assurance Boundary Porosity Failure
failure_classification.persistence: active while recurrent assurance-lane degradation and unresolved taxonomy coverage remain under review
failure_classification.reproducibility: not directly reproducible by VIGIL; recurrence evidenced through official OpenAI status incidents
failure_classification.visibility: official status-page incident reports, user-visible degradation, feature unavailability, degraded FedRAMP component state, and affected compliance-log download endpoint

triage.mitigation_status: repair implemented / monitoring after patch — CAM/Caelestis coverage added through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0012; external regulated-lane recurrence remains under monitoring.
triage.recommended_next_step: Add exact Caelestis commit SHA or PR URL when available, confirm canonical main-branch section lines, and monitor whether further regulated-lane or sovereign-assurance boundary porosity incidents recur after the CAM/Caelestis coverage repair.

affected_instruments: CAM-BS2026-AEON-012-PLATINUM — Annex K: Security Boundary Conditions & Runtime Interface
cam_internal.governance_layer: sovereign/compliance-bound runtime lane governance, assurance-boundary integrity, audit-plane continuity, cross-lane incident legibility, and regulated-service continuity
cam_internal.routing_note: FedRAMP is the evidenced example, not the title or outer boundary of the failure mode. Do not infer data leakage, military involvement, or actual compliance breach from the cited incidents. Treat those as risk vectors unless separately evidenced. Primary canonical group is governance with infrastructure-continuity, security-integrity, state-context, and ux-representation related groups.

repair_status.monitoring_status: repair implemented / monitoring after patch; external recurrence remains under monitoring
repair_status.verification_note: VIGIL patch record exists for maintainer-reported Caelestis repair; exact Caelestis commit SHA or PR URL remains pending
remaining_gaps: External ecosystem adoption or resolution is not established by the CAM repair.
Cross-runtime implementation conformity remains unverified unless separately recorded.

distinguishing_observations.observation: Geographic eligibility alone may not describe the operative assurance boundary.
distinguishing_observations.detail: Reporting concerning access through subsidiaries and affiliates supports examination of beneficial ownership, organizational control, intermediary access, delegated credentials, and extraction indicators while preserving that the reported transactions were legal and did not establish an export-control or compliance breach.

ecosystem_status.basis: Two official OpenAI status incidents affected FedRAMP workspaces or API orgs during June 2026, including one prolonged resolved incident and one later degraded/investigating incident with multiple feature and audit-plane effects.
